model.label: Grok 4.2
model.description: xAI • Large reasoning model designed for real-time information access, coding, and general problem solving.

pick.rationale: Florida holds the edge with No. 4 rankings in both KenPom and Torvik versus Vanderbilt's 10-12 range, driven by elite top-5 defense that should limit Vanderbilt's high-powered offense more effectively than in their recent SEC tournament meeting. The Gators' 26-7 record comes from the same tough SEC slate as Vanderbilt's, and while the Commodores' 91-74 blowout win in the semifinals gives the underdog a credible path via hot shooting and turnovers, it appears somewhat anomalous given Florida's prior victory and superior season-long metrics. No major injuries reported for either team, tilting the neutral-site rematch toward the higher-quality Gators despite the upset risk.

reasoningStep.title: Ratings, Recent H2H, and Upset Risk Assessment
reasoningStep.summary: Florida favored on advanced metrics and defensive strength despite Vanderbilt's recent dominant win; underdog has credible but not probable path.
evidence: KenPom/Torvik: Florida #4 (adjEM +33.8, adjD 91.8) vs Vanderbilt #12 (adjEM +28.1, adjD 99.2)
Both 26-7; Vandy won SEC semi 91-74 but Florida won regular-season meeting
No key injuries found in searches

result.text: Takeaways from Florida basketball's SEC Tournament loss vs Vanderbilt Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Baseball Recruiting SEC Sports Odds Newsletter GatorSports.com College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Takeaways from Florida basketball's SEC Tournament loss vs Vanderbilt Aidan Gallardo Gators Wire Florida basketball had no answers for the Vanderbilt Commodores in the SEC Tournament semifinals on Saturday. Head coach Todd Golden and the Gators knew this one wasn't going to be easy. The last time these two met, Florida needed a comeback in the second half to escape Nashville with a 98-94 win. This time, Vanderbilt, the No. 4 seed in the SEC Tournament, defeated the top-seeded Gators 91-74 inside Bridgestone Arena in Nashville, Tennessee. The Commodores shot 55% from the field and 48% from beyond the arc while forcing Florida into 14 turnovers. The Gators fought back on the glass, outrebounding Vanderbilt 38-23, but it wasn't enough to overcome a Vanderbilt offense

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
